requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible
User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes
User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions
User can see a button or option to create a new note
New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy
Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes
Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available
User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)
App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: In the image, there is a hierarchical list of notes visible on the screen. The notes are organized into categories with sub-categories. However, it's not clear if all notes are accessible or visible in this view as the screenshot only shows a partial section of the interface.
results.notes: The note hierarchy is partially displayed and only two layers (primary and secondary) can be seen. It is unclear from this image alone whether there are additional levels or notes that may not be visible on the screen.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there are notes displayed on the screen along with their titles. However, there is only one note visible which might not be enough to conclude if all notes are displayed with their titles visible.
results.notes: To fully evaluate this requirement, more than one note should be visible in the view.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.evidence: There is no visible sidebar or navigation for browsing notes on this screenshot.
results.notes: To determine if the requirement is met, we would need to see a different screen/state that displays the notes feature in the iOS app. The current image does not provide enough information to make a conclusive assessment.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a search bar visible at the top of the screen, labeled 'APPLICATION TEST'. This search bar is the primary element associated with the requirement of providing access to a search function.
results.notes: The design suggests that this could be a login or account setup flow which might not necessarily have a live search function. However, the presence of the search bar indicates it's part of the user interface, and it is likely functional.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: A search input field is clearly visible in the screenshot.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a smartphone interface with an AI chat or assistant feature visible as an app icon within the application being used.
results.notes: While there is no direct interaction with the AI chat or assistant in the current state of the UI, its presence on the home screen indicates that it is accessible. The chat or assistant icon is clearly identifiable and labeled.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is an input field for questions at the bottom of the screen. It appears to be a chat interface where users can type messages or send questions. The presence of this input field suggests that it's meant for user interaction and therefore meets the requirement.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: No button or option to create a new note is visible in this screenshot.
results.notes: It's possible that there is another screen or interaction required to access the 'Create Note' functionality. However, based on the visible content, it appears that such an action cannot be performed from this particular view.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: There are multiple options available on the main screen (Today, Awaiting Test, Done), but there is no clear indication of a 'Create Note' option.
results.notes: The app seems to have a note creation feature accessible from other screens. It would be useful to test if this is also true for the main screen.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile app with a section titled 'Test Task'. There are sub-items under 'Test Task' indicating that there is support for nesting or hierarchy in the notes. This suggests that the requirement of notes supporting nesting or hierarchy has been met.
results.notes: From what can be seen, it appears that the app allows users to create a hierarchical structure within their notes. The section titled 'Test Task' serves as a parent note, and there are sub-items under it, which would allow for a more detailed breakdown of tasks or ideas.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: The screenshot shows an iPhone screen displaying a messaging app with nested notes. The indentation for the nested note is not visible in this image.
results.notes: To fully assess if the nesting requirement is met, it would be necessary to view different states of the app or have access to additional features and functionalities of the app. This screenshot only shows one specific state of a conversation within the messaging app.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: The image shows a screenshot of an iOS app in the editing mode for notes. There is a note selected and a text box with input fields that can be used to edit or add new text.
results.notes: While it is clear that there is an area where one can input text (the text box), it is not possible to determine from this screenshot if the editor area is visible when viewing a note. It would require more information, such as whether the text box is always visible or if it can be hidden or made less prominent, to make a conclusive assessment.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: No formatting toolbar or options are visible in the provided screenshot. The image shows a notification from an app, but there is no visible toolbar or formatting options for editing the text of the notification.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.evidence: The screenshot shows an iOS app displaying a user interface where there is clear block-level content, including paragraphs and headings that can be edited in the block editor. The content on this screen appears to be well-structured with visible formatting options such as headings, subheadings, and lists.
results.notes: Additional elements like images and other media are also present in the screenshot, but they do not seem to be part of the requirement.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is no visible mobile-appropriate navigation such as bottom tabs or a hamburger menu.
results.notes: The navigation appears to be in the form of a list of items at the top of the screen. This may not be considered a mobile-appropriate navigation since it does not adhere to the standard design guidelines for iOS apps, which typically use bottom tabs or a hamburger menu.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: The buttons on the screen are of a reasonable size and appear to be appropriately spaced for easy touch interaction.
results.notes: It is important to consider if this is representative of all screens within the app, but based on this snapshot, the requirement seems to be met.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: The screenshot shows that the content on the screen is not overflowing the viewport horizontally. It's fully contained within the visible area of the smartphone screen.
results.notes: The text is well-organized and spaced out, which ensures readability without horizontal scrolling.
